title: The Gang Gets Trapped

jokes.text: This is as low as I go, okay? I say we bum-rush her and choke her out with her own belt.
jokes.setup: Dennis asks Dee to lower her voice in what should be a quiet situation
jokes.punchline: Dee immediately escalates to murder while claiming she's being quiet
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation comedy - Dee immediately jumps to violence while missing Dennis's point entirely. Great character work showing how they completely miss normal social cues.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: This is as low as I go, okay? I say we bum-rush her and choke her out with her own belt.

jokes.text: We immediately escalate everything to a ten. It's ridiculous. Nobody's on the brakes. Nobody's thinking.
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizes the gang's tendency to escalate situations
jokes.punchline: He's doing this while literally trapped in someone's house after breaking in
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy where Dennis gains self-awareness about their terrible decision-making while in the middle of their worst decision yet.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We immediately escalate everything to a ten. It's ridiculous. Nobody's on the brakes. Nobody's thinking.

jokes.text: Dee, your breath is hideous. I mean, it smells bad.
jokes.setup: They're trapped in a tense situation
jokes.punchline: Dennis focuses on Dee's bad breath instead of their predicament
jokes.explanation: Dennis's vanity and superficiality even in crisis situations. The clarification 'I mean, it smells bad' adds unnecessary cruelty.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dee, your breath is hideous. I mean, it smells bad.

jokes.text: I shouldn't have stood on that table and given that awesome speech about how I was the perfect one to lead us into the house because of my wonderful grace.
jokes.setup: Dennis is showing regret about their situation
jokes.punchline: He calls his own speech 'awesome' while regretting it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - even while admitting fault, he can't help but compliment himself on his speech delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I shouldn't have stood on that table and given that awesome speech about how I was the perfect one to lead us into the house because of my wonderful grace.

jokes.text: I definitely regret you getting quadruple onions on your burger.
jokes.setup: Dennis continues listing regrets about their situation
jokes.punchline: He focuses on Dee's food choice causing her bad breath
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the breath issue while showing Dennis's petty priorities even in crisis.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I definitely regret you getting quadruple onions on your burger.

jokes.text: You nosed your way into this like you do everything. You are nothing but liabilities, Dee.
jokes.setup: Dee claims she was dragged into this situation
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out she inserted herself, calling her 'liabilities' (plural)
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis-Dee dynamic. The grammar error of 'liabilities' (treating Dee as multiple problems) adds to the insult.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You nosed your way into this like you do everything. You are nothing but liabilities, Dee.

jokes.text: Have you recovered the artifact yet? It belongs in a museum.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie are taking their Indiana Jones roleplay very seriously
jokes.punchline: They're treating a home invasion like an archaeological expedition
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist comedy where they've completely bought into their movie fantasy while committing actual crimes.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Have you recovered the artifact yet? It belongs in a museum.

jokes.text: Indiana Jones is a dumb movie?
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizes their movie-based plan
jokes.punchline: Mac is genuinely offended about the movie criticism, not the criminal activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect misplaced priorities - Mac is more upset about movie criticism than being trapped during a felony.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Indiana Jones is a dumb movie?

jokes.text: I have the grace of a falcon, and I'll be in and out like a demon's whisper
jokes.setup: Dennis is recounting his earlier boastful speech
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous poetic language contrasts with their current trapped state
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd Dennis dialogue - the overwrought metaphors perfectly capture his delusional self-image. Highly quotable and character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I have the grace of a falcon, and I'll be in and out like a demon's whisper

jokes.text: It was very persuasive. But I regret it now. I got to pee.
jokes.setup: Dennis admits his speech was persuasive
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts the moment by needing to urinate
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Dennis's grandiosity with a basic bodily function, bringing him back to reality.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It was very persuasive. But I regret it now. I got to pee.

jokes.text: We should not be committing crimes based on beliefs that are two hours old.
jokes.setup: Dennis is trying to be the voice of reason
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific timeframe makes it funnier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that perfectly captures the gang's impulsive stupidity. The precision of 'two hours old' makes it absurdly funny and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We should not be committing crimes based on beliefs that are two hours old.

jokes.text: There is a man getting out. The subject appears to have pointed feet of some kind.
jokes.setup: Charlie is providing surveillance intel
jokes.punchline: His observation about 'pointed feet' is bizarre and unhelpful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie weirdness - taking spy roleplay seriously but providing completely useless information in his unique strange way.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: There is a man getting out. The subject appears to have pointed feet of some kind.

jokes.text: He's wearing cowboy boots. His feet could be pointed on the inside, so let's be... They're definitely not pointed on the inside.
jokes.setup: Charlie clarifies the man is wearing cowboy boots
jokes.punchline: He continues to obsess over whether the feet themselves are pointed
jokes.explanation: Charlie's inability to let go of irrelevant details, even when correcting himself. Classic Charlie obsessive behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He's wearing cowboy boots. His feet could be pointed on the inside, so let's be... They're definitely not pointed on the inside.

jokes.text: Yippee-i-ki-yay.
jokes.setup: Frank is getting back into the closet during the crisis
jokes.punchline: He quotes Die Hard for no apparent reason
jokes.explanation: Random Frank weirdness - inappropriate movie quote that makes no contextual sense, typical of his chaotic energy.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yippee-i-ki-yay.

jokes.text: Basically, these people are Southern, and the lady lied to the man.
jokes.setup: Charlie asks what's happening
jokes.punchline: Dennis reduces a complex domestic situation to regional stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Dennis's tendency to oversimplify and stereotype, reducing a nuanced situation to geographic prejudice.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Basically, these people are Southern, and the lady lied to the man.

jokes.text: People from the South don't usually move up North. Oh, no, they come up. These carpetbaggers come up by the truckloads just to steal our artifacts.
jokes.setup: Mac makes a geographical observation
jokes.punchline: Charlie invents a conspiracy theory using Civil War terminology incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Great Charlie conspiracy thinking - using 'carpetbaggers' completely wrong while creating elaborate theories from nothing.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: People from the South don't usually move up North. Oh, no, they come up. These carpetbaggers come up by the truckloads just to steal our artifacts.

jokes.text: These Southerners, they probably pray for a home invasion every single day.
jokes.setup: Dennis is escalating fears about being caught
jokes.punchline: He imagines Southerners religiously hoping for violence
jokes.explanation: Dark regional stereotyping that reveals Dennis's own paranoia while being absurdly specific about Southern prayer habits.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: These Southerners, they probably pray for a home invasion every single day.

jokes.text: You're not getting shit, okay?! If you wanted chips, you could've gotten a bag at the hamburger store.
jokes.setup: Charlie keeps asking for chips
jokes.punchline: Mac has an explosive breakdown over snack food during a home invasion
jokes.explanation: Great misplaced anger - Mac getting genuinely furious about chips while committing felonies. 'Hamburger store' adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You're not getting shit, okay?! If you wanted chips, you could've gotten a bag at the hamburger store.

jokes.text: She's banging Leno. If she was banging Jay Leno, why would she have to buy a bunch of tickets to his show?
jokes.setup: Frank theorizes about Jay Leno tickets
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out the logical flaw in Frank's celebrity affair theory
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist comedy with Frank creating wild theories and Dennis applying logic to the illogical.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: She's banging Leno.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: If she was banging Jay Leno, why would she have to buy a bunch of tickets to his show?

jokes.text: She's trying to kill him. She's still very upset about the whole Conan thing.
jokes.setup: Dennis rejects Frank's theory
jokes.punchline: He creates an even more elaborate assassination theory based on late-night TV drama
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Dennis creates an even more absurd theory, tying it to real late-night TV controversy for topical humor.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: She's trying to kill him. She's still very upset about the whole Conan thing.

jokes.text: Those are the theories of lunatics.
jokes.setup: Dennis has just proposed an assassination theory
jokes.punchline: He immediately calls conspiracy theories lunatic ideas after creating one himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis hypocrisy - zero self-awareness about his own contribution to the lunacy he's criticizing.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Those are the theories of lunatics.

jokes.text: Never should have let him have that whip.
jokes.setup: Frank is apparently causing chaos with a whip
jokes.punchline: The casual admission that they gave Frank a weapon during a robbery
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy inference - Frank with a whip is inherently dangerous, and this suggests ongoing chaos.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Never should have let him have that whip.

jokes.text: Dee, you gangly, uncoordinated bitch, I am not getting hogtied over your lack of grace.
jokes.setup: Dennis is frustrated with Dee's movement
jokes.punchline: Specific physical insults followed by Southern torture fears
jokes.explanation: Classic Dennis cruelty with specific body shaming, connecting to his earlier boasts about grace and Southern paranoia.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dee, you gangly, uncoordinated bitch, I am not getting hogtied over your lack of grace.

jokes.text: I hope they hogtie you. And then I hope they rape you in their basement for ten years.
jokes.setup: Dee is angry at Dennis's insults
jokes.punchline: She wishes elaborate Southern torture on him
jokes.explanation: Very dark escalation showing Dee's viciousness, though it relies heavily on shock value rather than clever construction.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I hope they hogtie you. And then I hope they rape you in their basement for ten years.

jokes.text: You know what? I'll be a plumber. I'll knock on the front door, tell 'em I got a deal on pipes, you guys run out the back.
jokes.setup: Mac needs to create a diversion
jokes.punchline: His plan is to randomly offer plumbing deals to strangers
jokes.explanation: Mac's bizarre understanding of how diversions work - the randomness of offering pipe deals is perfectly absurd.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You know what? I'll be a plumber. I'll knock on the front door, tell 'em I got a deal on pipes, you guys run out the back.

jokes.text: Ooh, I'll do a Swedish accent!
jokes.setup: Mac has a simple plumber plan
jokes.punchline: He unnecessarily complicates it with an ethnic accent
jokes.explanation: Mac's tendency to overcomplicate simple plans with unnecessary theatrical elements. Shows his childlike excitement.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Ooh, I'll do a Swedish accent!

jokes.text: the tasseled leathers, the turquoise studs on the leathers. All the leather.
jokes.setup: Mac mentioning the leather shop
jokes.punchline: Charlie's enthusiastic specificity about their ridiculous business plan
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that shows how detailed their fantasy is. Charlie's enthusiasm is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: the tasseled leathers, the turquoise studs on the leathers. All the leather.

jokes.text: I'm a Swedish plumber— I'm here to fix your pipes. That's good. That's a good accent.
jokes.setup: Mac practices his Swedish plumber character
jokes.punchline: He compliments his own terrible accent
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac self-congratulation on something he's terrible at. The audience can infer the accent is bad from his praise.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm a Swedish plumber— I'm here to fix your pipes. That's good. That's a good accent.

jokes.text: There are far too many leather shops in Arizona as it is. You would be out of business in a week's time.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie fantasized about opening a leather shop
jokes.punchline: Dennis provides practical business analysis during a crime
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan response where Dennis provides serious market analysis to their ridiculous escape fantasy.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: There are far too many leather shops in Arizona as it is. You would be out of business in a week's time.

jokes.text: You got us all freaked out in here, sitting here waiting for the Southerners to tie us up to horses and then have them run in opposite directions, ripping us apart.
jokes.setup: Mac is defending their escape fantasies
jokes.punchline: He describes an elaborate medieval torture scenario
jokes.explanation: Mac's imagination for Southern torture has escalated to historical execution methods. Dark but somewhat repetitive.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You got us all freaked out in here, sitting here waiting for the Southerners to tie us up to horses and then have them run in opposite directions, ripping us apart.

jokes.text: If you wanted the goddamn chami, you should have gotten the goddamn chips at the hamburger shop!
jokes.setup: Charlie still wants chips
jokes.punchline: Mac's explosive rage includes mispronouncing 'chips' as 'chami'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Mac so angry he's mispronouncing words. The 'hamburger shop' phrasing adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: If you wanted the goddamn chami, you should have gotten the goddamn chips at the hamburger shop!

jokes.text: What if this vase is cursed, okay? Get Dee to touch it.
jokes.setup: Charlie worries about a cursed vase
jokes.punchline: His solution is to sacrifice Dee to the curse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie logic - accepting supernatural curses as real but casually sacrificing friends. Dark and economical.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What if this vase is cursed, okay? Get Dee to touch it.

jokes.text: It was a time of science.
jokes.setup: Dennis argues the vase isn't cursed because it's from the 1800s
jokes.punchline: He calls the 1800s 'a time of science' as if that prevents curses
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dennis pseudo-intellectualism. The phrase 'time of science' is perfectly absurd and highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: It was a time of science.

jokes.text: How come Mac's the only one who gets to push the button on the walkie?
jokes.setup: Dee is trying to split up Mac and Charlie
jokes.punchline: She appeals to Charlie's childish jealousy over walkie-talkie control
jokes.explanation: Good Dee manipulation tactic, appealing to Charlie's childlike need for fairness over toy privileges.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: How come Mac's the only one who gets to push the button on the walkie?

jokes.text: The little bitch has got a suitcase. Frilly little dress, pink nightie.
jokes.setup: Frank is investigating a child's room
jokes.punchline: He refers to a little girl as 'the little bitch' while describing her innocent items
jokes.explanation: Dark Frank humor showing his complete lack of appropriate boundaries with children. Shock value over craft.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: The little bitch has got a suitcase. Frilly little dress, pink nightie.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Albert, I love you.
jokes.setup: Frank has damaged a talking teddy bear
jokes.punchline: The bear repeatedly declares love during a home invasion
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment where innocent children's toy creates chaos during criminal activity. The contrast is effective.

dialogue.speaker: Teddy Bear
dialogue.line: Hi, I'm Albert, I love you.

jokes.text: I snuck in. Mac was, like, being a dick about the walkie-talkie.
jokes.setup: Charlie appears in the house unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: He abandoned his post over a petty dispute about radio privileges
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie pettiness - he infiltrated the house over toy jealousy, showing how easily manipulated he is.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I snuck in. Mac was, like, being a dick about the walkie-talkie.

jokes.text: Can you go ahead and throw an extra double cheeseburger on there, please? 'Cause I'm very hungry.
jokes.setup: Mac is supposed to be helping with their emergency
jokes.punchline: He's ordering food while they're trapped
jokes.explanation: Mac's selfish priorities during crisis - his hunger matters more than their emergency situation.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Can you go ahead and throw an extra double cheeseburger on there, please? 'Cause I'm very hungry.

jokes.text: I'm a Swedish plumber— I'm here to fix your pipes. I'm gonna do a Swedish accent, because that's what Indy would do.
jokes.setup: Mac explains his diversion plan
jokes.punchline: He claims Indiana Jones would use a Swedish plumber disguise
jokes.explanation: Mac's complete misunderstanding of Indiana Jones methodology combined with his accent obsession.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I'm a Swedish plumber— I'm here to fix your pipes. I'm gonna do a Swedish accent, because that's what Indy would do.

jokes.text: You just walked right in the front door? Well, I ran, I had a little hustle going, but yeah, front door.
jokes.setup: Dennis can't believe Charlie's entry method
jokes.punchline: Charlie describes casual jogging as if it made breaking in more professional
jokes.explanation: Charlie's casual attitude toward crime - 'a little hustle' makes it sound like exercise rather than breaking and entering.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: You just walked right in the front door?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, I ran, I had a little hustle going, but yeah, front door.

jokes.text: I'm gonna whip this little bitch in the face if she makes a peep.
jokes.setup: A child is approaching Frank's hiding spot
jokes.punchline: Frank threatens violence against a small child
jokes.explanation: Dark Frank humor but relies mainly on shock value of threatening a child. Less craft than previous child-related comments.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I'm gonna whip this little bitch in the face if she makes a peep.

jokes.text: We are assholes. This is a nice woman planning a surprise California vacation.
jokes.setup: Dennis realizes they've misjudged the family completely
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of self-awareness about their terrible behavior
jokes.explanation: Rare Dennis moment of genuine self-reflection. The simple declaration 'We are assholes' is both honest and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: We are assholes. This is a nice woman planning a surprise California vacation.

jokes.text: Well, I'll be your new daddy.
jokes.setup: Barry is revealed to be having an affair
jokes.punchline: He immediately tries to replace the father figure to the child
jokes.explanation: Dark cringe moment where the real family drama is more uncomfortable than the gang's imagined scenarios.

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: Well, I'll be your new daddy.

jokes.text: Barry, I followed you, you son of a bitch. I knew something was up. You're my best friend!
jokes.setup: The husband catches the affair
jokes.punchline: The betrayal involves his best friend, making it worse
jokes.explanation: Real family drama that's more intense than the gang's paranoid fantasies. The best friend betrayal adds layers.

dialogue.line: Barry, I followed you, you son of a bitch. I knew something was up. You're my best friend!

jokes.text: Oh, God, no. All night? I can't do all night, you guys. I don't have it in me.
jokes.setup: The husband wants to talk all night to resolve their marriage
jokes.punchline: Dennis treats it like a personal endurance challenge
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic response to someone else's marriage crisis - making it about his own comfort and stamina.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, God, no. All night? I can't do all night, you guys. I don't have it in me.

jokes.text: Hello! I am the Swedish plumber... Uh, Mac... Hey, Frank, we're out here!
jokes.setup: Mac arrives to do his Swedish plumber diversion
jokes.punchline: He immediately breaks character and blows everyone's cover
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac incompetence - he abandons his elaborate plan immediately and exposes everyone. Great payoff to the setup.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Hello! I am the Swedish plumber... Uh, Mac... Hey, Frank, we're out here!

jokes.text: It was weird that they were Asian, right?
jokes.setup: They've escaped and are processing what happened
jokes.punchline: Mac focuses on irrelevant racial observation after all their paranoid Southern theories
jokes.explanation: Brilliant final punchline that completely undermines all their regional stereotyping and paranoid theories. Perfect Mac obliviousness.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It was weird that they were Asian, right?

tmdb_overview: The Gang's plan to "extract" an "artifact" from a residence is jeopardized when the owners turn out to be home. Having not been noticed on the way in, they must make a plan to get the same result for the way out.
